message:
Last weekend my kid asked why we were stuffing a bag with flashlights and snacks, and honestly I almost gave the whole 'just in case' speech. Instead I said, 'Want to help me build our family adventure kit?' Suddenly it turned into this cozy thing—we picked out their favorite granola bars, they drew labels for the water bottles, and we made a whole game of it.

I think that's when it clicked for me: getting ready doesn't have to feel heavy or scary. It's just... taking care of each other, and sometimes that's actually kind of nice.

Does anyone else find it helps to reframe prep stuff as something more positive instead of doom-and-gloom?
